items.briefing:
Cyber threats across the Asia-Pacific region are increasing, driven by artificial intelligence and geopolitical tensions — a trend directly affecting Australian organisations. Locally, a new tool called PhishByte has highlighted that the government's Essential Eight cybersecurity framework may not adequately protect against phishing attacks, meaning staff clicking malicious links remains a significant gap. Australian businesses are responding by moving toward unified security platforms to simplify their defences.

Separately, a critical vulnerability called WP2Shell is putting millions of WordPress websites at risk of remote takeover — relevant to any Australian organisation running a WordPress site. Ensure your WordPress plugins and core software are updated immediately.

featured.title: PhishByte warns Essential Eight misses phishing risk
featured.quote: Essential Eight compliance doesn't guarantee protection against phishing, Australia's primary cyber threat.
